description: Models and tools for understanding periodicity, structure, bonding and reactivity. In the tutorials examples illustrate concepts and relevant spectroscopy.
preAndCorequisites:
• Complete all of:
    • CHEM102 - Chemical Reactivity Fundamentals with Environmental Applications (1.5)
supplementalNotes:
• Credit will be granted for only one of CHEM 222, CHEM 225.
  • Students with credit in CHEM 232 should register in CHEM 222.
title: Introduction to Inorganic Chemistry
